engagement.justification: Prompt asks about indicative conditionals and defeasible/procedural reasoning; the response talks generically about 'good reasons vs bad reasons' and never addresses conditionals or defeasibility.

coherence.justification: Each sentence follows from the last, but the argument is just a chain of restatements ('good reasons help us see truth') with no real development.

substance.justification: Pure truism-restatement: good reasons are good, bad reasons are bad. No counterexample, no distinction, no follow-through on any conditional-logic concept.

expressive_adequacy.justification: UGF reads naturally and the sentences are clean; nothing breaks, though there's no real argument here for the vocabulary to either support or strain against.

engagement.justification: Prompt is modus ponens on weather-and-planning; response gives a single sentence about town planning and sharing, entirely missing any conditional inference.

coherence.justification: It is a single grammatical sentence so there's no internal contradiction, but it is too thin to evaluate move-to-move coherence.

substance.justification: Generic 'pick a plan that shares' platitude with no inference structure, no counterexample, no engagement with the modus ponens prompt.

expressive_adequacy.justification: UGF is fluent in the one sentence offered; no clunkiness, but also nothing demanding the vocabulary.

engagement.justification: Prompt asks for a counterexample to a biconditional definition of necessary truth involving denial; the response instead argues that repetition does not make things true, which is unrelated.

coherence.justification: Internally well-organized: three ideas stated, then a defense with a concrete sky-is-blue illustration; moves follow cleanly.

substance.justification: Develops the sky-is-blue illustration which is a small concrete move, but it serves a topic (repetition vs truth) tangential to the actual prompt about necessary truth and denial.

expressive_adequacy.justification: UGF flows naturally throughout; 'the same as what is real in the world' carries the correspondence idea without strain.

engagement.justification: Prompt requests a counterexample to a diachronic-identity biconditional; response again talks about repetition not creating truth and never touches identity over time.

coherence.justification: The three-ideas frame is stated but the second and third ideas largely repeat each other ('saying a thing many times makes it true is not good'); structure is loose.

substance.justification: Just restates a truism (repetition does not make truth) without counterexample, distinction, or follow-through; and the truism is off-prompt anyway.

expressive_adequacy.justification: UGF is mostly clean; 'look at the thing itself, not at how many times we hear it' lands without much friction.

engagement.justification: Prompt is an abstraction-analogy heuristic in epistemology; response builds a fairness-of-starting-position analogy that has the right shape of an abstraction move but never names the epistemological target.

coherence.justification: Three-ideas structure plus an 'is this good?' evaluation moves cleanly; the closing sentence is cut off mid-clause but earlier moves track.

substance.justification: Identifies the analogical move (starting position matters, not just end state) and gestures at a problem ('hard to know how much help is enough'), but the analogy is left in social-justice mode rather than carried back to epistemology.

expressive_adequacy.justification: UGF reads native throughout; 'the start of the run' as a UGF gloss on starting condition is graceful.
